top_5_findings.title: Risky Port Listening: 445 (SMB)
top_5_findings.detail: Port 445 is open: SMB — verify patched against EternalBlue/MS17-010. Verify this service is intentionally exposed and access is restricted.
top_5_findings.recommendation: If not required externally, add a firewall rule blocking port 445. If the service must be accessible, enforce authentication and encryption.

top_5_findings.title: Risky Port Listening: 3389 (RDP)
top_5_findings.detail: Port 3389 is open: RDP — ensure NLA enabled, restrict via firewall. Verify this service is intentionally exposed and access is restricted.
top_5_findings.recommendation: If not required externally, add a firewall rule blocking port 3389. If the service must be accessible, enforce authentication and encryption.

top_5_findings.title: Windows Defender Real-Time Protection Disabled
top_5_findings.detail: Windows Defender real-time protection is disabled. (RealTimeProtection=False, AntivirusEnabled=False). The system is unprotected against known malware.
top_5_findings.recommendation: Re-enable Windows Defender via Settings → Windows Security → Virus & Threat Protection. If a third-party AV is installed, ensure it is active and up-to-date.

top_5_findings.title: Defender Signatures Outdated (4294967295 Days Old)
top_5_findings.detail: Antivirus signatures are 4294967295 days old. Signatures older than 7 days leave the system exposed to recent malware variants.
top_5_findings.recommendation: Update Defender signatures immediately: Update-MpSignature (PowerShell) or Windows Security → Check for Updates.

top_5_findings.title: Risky Port Listening: 135 (RPC endpoint mapper)
top_5_findings.detail: Port 135 is open: RPC endpoint mapper — restrict via firewall. Verify this service is intentionally exposed and access is restricted.
top_5_findings.recommendation: If not required externally, add a firewall rule blocking port 135. If the service must be accessible, enforce authentication and encryption.
